eml_analysis.name: koch_curve
eml_analysis.insight: Koch subdivision: each segment becomes 4 via scale(1/3) + rotate(60°). Rotation uses cos(60°)=0.5 (exact), sin(60°)=√3/2 (irrational constant). Full step is EML-2 (scale·complex multiply = degree-2 poly in Re/Im). Limit curve: 4^n segments → ∞. EML-inf limit (infinite self-similar detail). Hausdorff dim = log(4)/log(3) ≈ 1.2619.

eml_analysis.name: mandelbrot_set
eml_analysis.insight: Mandelbrot: z_{n+1} = z_n² + c. In real coordinates: (x,y) -> (x²-y², 2xy) + (Re(c),Im(c)). Quadratic map → EML-2 per step. SET MEMBERSHIP is a limit (n→∞) → EML-inf to determine. Boundary is fractal (Hausdorff dim=2) → EML-inf geometry. The MAP is EML-2; the OBJECT (set) is EML-inf.

eml_analysis.name: julia_set_c=(-0.7269+0.1889j)
eml_analysis.connected: If c in Mandelbrot set; disconnected (Cantor) if c outside
eml_analysis.insight: Julia set for c=(-0.7269+0.1889j): same z→z²+c iteration as Mandelbrot. EML-2 per step. The SET is EML-inf (fractal boundary). If c is in the Mandelbrot set, J_c is connected; else it's a Cantor dust (totally disconnected, EML-inf in the Cantor set sense).

sierpinski.name: sierpinski_triangle
sierpinski.insight: IFS 'sierpinski_triangle': 3 affine maps, each EML-1. Attractor has Hausdorff dim=1.5850. The MAP is EML-1 per step; the LIMIT SET is EML-inf (fractal). Self-similarity requires infinitely many compositions.

sierpinski_triangle.verdict: EML-1 per affine step; fractal limit is EML-inf

koch_curve.verdict: EML-2 per subdivision (rotation+scale); limit is EML-inf

mandelbrot_set.verdict: EML-2 per step z->z²+c; set boundary is EML-inf (Hausdorff dim=2)

julia_set.verdict: EML-2 per step; connected/Cantor depending on c

cantor_set.verdict: EML-1 per scale step (multiply by 1/3); Cantor limit is EML-inf

barnsley_fern.verdict: EML-1 per affine step; fern shape is EML-inf limit

key_insight: Universal fractal theorem (EML version): A fractal F generated by an EML-k iteration has EML-k per step but EML-inf geometry (the limit set). The gap between 'step complexity' and 'attractor complexity' is infinite. This is the fractal analogue of: pure tone (EML-3) vs noise (EML-inf). The iteration is analytic; infinite repetition produces non-analyticity.

summary.title: Fractals — EML Complexity of Fractal Geometry

findings.name: Universal Fractal EML Theorem
findings.content: EML-k map + infinite iteration = EML-inf attractor. Gap is always infinite.

findings.name: Mandelbrot = EML-2 per step, EML-inf boundary
findings.content: z->z²+c is EML-2. Boundary has Hausdorff dim 2 (Shishikura 1998). Set membership requires infinite iterations → EML-inf.

findings.name: Koch curve EML-2 per subdivision
findings.content: Rotation by 60° uses cos/sin constants. Full step is EML-2. Limit curve is EML-inf (infinite segments).

findings.name: IFS attractors all EML-inf
findings.content: Every IFS uses EML-1 affine maps. All IFS attractors are EML-inf (self-similar → infinite detail).

findings.name: Connection to Chaos (Session 51)
findings.content: Smooth chaotic maps (EML-finite) can produce fractal attractors. The map stays EML-finite; the attractor is EML-inf. Same gap as fractal generators.

next_session.title: Bio/Chemical Networks — EML Complexity
priorities: Lotka-Volterra: degree-2 polynomial → EML-2 per step
Turing patterns: reaction-diffusion, EML-2 RHS + Laplacian
SIR epidemic model: EML-2 (bilinear infection term S*I)
